claimText: The IUCN status of the Greater One-Horned Rhino (Indian Rhino) is Vulnerable.
examUse: Statement-based question on species conservation status

claimText: Assam holds the world's largest population of the Greater One-Horned Rhino, with over 90% located in Kaziranga National Park.
evidenceExcerpt: Assam has the world's largest population. More than 90% of them are in Kaziranga National Park.
distractorHooks: West Bengal holds the largest population
Manas National Park holds over 90% of the population

claimText: The Indian Rhino is the largest of all Asian rhino species.
evidenceExcerpt: Indian Rhino is the largest of all Asian rhino species.
distractorHooks: Sumatran rhino is the largest Asian rhino
Javan rhino is the largest Asian rhino

claimText: The Sumatran and Javan rhinos are both listed as Critically Endangered.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching species to IUCN status

claimText: Among African rhinos, the White Rhino is Near Threatened while the Black Rhino is Critically Endangered.
evidenceExcerpt: 2 African: White Rhino (Near Threatened), Black Rhino (Critically Endangered).
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on African rhino statuses

claimText: RhoDIS India is a DNA data library used for profiling rhino horns.
evidenceExcerpt: Assam Forest Department started DNA profiling of Rhino horns and add it to Rhino DNA Index System (RhoDIS) India DNA data library.
distractorHooks: RhoDIS is a satellite tracking system for rhinos
RhoDIS is an international anti-poaching task force
